methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DIRAC is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Fil Aronshtein that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader peer universe of 9 private robotics companies and 1 public comparable, including firms like 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is applicable and anchors the valuation framework. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $12M. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector—while 11 peers exist in the sector broadly, fewer than three meet tight filtration criteria, making narrow peer multiples unreliable.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct stage-matched comparables to validate the 2.5x step-up multiplier and the heterogeneity of the broader peer set in business model, geography, and technology focus. Additional data on DIRAC's unit economics, customer pipeline, or recent funding terms would strengthen conviction in this range.
